strengths: Built and deployed production-grade agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators supporting real-world agent workflows, directly aligning with the need for tool-augmented, stateful agents beyond prompt chaining.

Demonstrated deep backend and distributed systems expertise across high-throughput environments (Optilyz, Dunnhumby) with real-time data handling, microservices, and low-latency systems using DLang, Node.js, and event-driven architectures.

Proven track record scaling engineering culture through TDD, CI/CD, pair programming, and open-source leadership — including founding GISCollective and creating developer tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) — mirroring the need for disciplined, high-leverage team growth and observability focus.
concerns: Current career gap since October 2025 raises questions about recent hands-on technical engagement and leadership continuity at the executive level.
No explicit evidence of direct experience with financial regulatory frameworks, credit-risk modeling, or working-capital systems — core to the fintech-infrastructure mandate.
Limited visibility into team leadership beyond individual contributor roles; no clear documentation of scaling teams from 10 to 30+ engineers or governing hybrid external engineering models.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, verifiable production experience building agentic AI infrastructure and high-throughput distributed systems — directly matching the core technical demands of the role — while demonstrating exceptional engineering discipline, tooling innovation, and culture-building through open-source leadership; his strongest gap is in fintech-specific regulatory context, but his proven ability to architect reliable, auditable, real-time systems in high-stakes environments suggests strong transferable upside.
